behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is local scoring logic (count/threshold/label), not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP: none; deterministic keyword rules. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly about one analyzer’s assignment logic only.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests over the analyzer function with controlled positive/negative lexicons and thresholds, asserting exact label outcomes for representative and edge-case token mixes.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on output quality/content (phrase grounding) from the explanation analyzer; stubs would hide this. REAL-INFRA is not the boundary; model judgment is. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, so not Workflow. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires semantic quality of generated reasoning, not just routing/format.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation against a fixed hard-case canary set (precondition gate) where each review has required source phrases; assert explanations quote or clearly reference review-specific spans and fail generic-template outputs across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior targets deterministic tie-break logic inside sentiment scoring. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because description is rule application within analyzer, not cross-module state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests for mixed-keyword inputs around tie boundaries, varying configuration to assert the selected tie-break policy is applied exactly (including equal-count and near-threshold cases).

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: sarcasm/intent detection is model judgment; deterministic mocks cannot validate it. REAL-INFRA is secondary to model output quality. MULTI-STEP: one agentic decision, not a multi-agent pipeline. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly tests interpretation quality under sarcasm.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test using a fixed sarcasm canary set (precondition gate) with expected intent labels/rationales; run repeated evaluations and require consistent negative-intent identification plus rationale tied to sarcastic cues.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration plus explanation-content alignment. Integration can verify response bundling, but would miss real mismatches if explanation is mocked. LLM-DEP applies because correctness includes explanation direction consistency against real generated text. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic generation step in this behavior, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end agentic service test over a fixed canary set: call real pipeline once per review, assert response contains both fields, and verify explanation polarity matches computed score direction (including contradiction checks) before broader corpus runs.
